mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 4.49% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevation of FEDFUNDS above 4.25% signals ongoing tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market participants reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 2276k units signal robust residential construction demand. Sustained HOUST above 2200k indicates strong housing-led economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction activity translates into broader economic growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when HOUST > 2200k, or correlation between HOUST and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.8% combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 134789k indicates tight labor market conditions. When UNRATE remains below 5.0% and PAYEMS shows month-over-month growth, this signals strong labor demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% and PAYEMS at 134789k as of 2006-02-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (UNRATE < 5.0% AND PAYEMS growth) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 110.269 reflects manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO below its 3-month moving average signals weakening production momentum and potential supply constraints that compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 58% of instances when INDPRO falls below 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) at 362294M and real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11247.6B jointly capture aggregate demand strength. When both RSAFS shows month-over-month growth and GDPC1 growth remains above 2.5% annualized, this signals sustained consumption and investment that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0 AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 199.0 combined with FEDFUNDS at 4.49% indicates the Fed is maintaining real rates in restrictive territory. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%, the Fed is unlikely to ease, sustaining headwinds for equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 64% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.8% represents near-full employment. A rise in UNRATE above 5.2% over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market prices in recession risk.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration leading to recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 61% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.4pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 2276k combined with real retail sales (RSAFS) at 362294M jointly capture consumer and residential demand. When HOUST declines below 2100k while RSAFS growth slows below 0.5% month-over-month, this signals demand-side weakness that portends earnings misses, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: demand-side deceleration in housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 59% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
